laws.title: Public Law 1-1 | Enactment of the U.S. Codes Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Foundational codification enactment; preserve as statutory history only.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; no superseding repeal or transfer identified.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal, transfer, or redesignation issue requiring operative text changes.
laws.rationale: The law functions as a foundational enactment note. The current XML should be concise and historical, without the quoted dump or unrelated link material.

laws.title: Public Law 1-2 | United States Bar Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Attorney General controls the United States Bar Exam, and the exam is defined by the Act.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; no later repeal identified in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer; the current Title 3 placement is incorrect for the operative rule.
laws.rationale: This is a permanent substantive rule, not a note-only history item. It belongs in operative Code text under the manifest target rather than as a historical note at Title 3.

laws.title: Public Law 1-3 | Closing Cabinet Doors Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Cabinet restructuring and department closures, including the veteran affairs transfer and blanket repeal coverage.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; no superseding repeal identified in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Includes both direct restructuring and blanket repeal language; section-level strikes are not a complete implementation.
laws.rationale: The source is a structural enactment with broad repeal consequences. Historical-note treatment is legally sufficient so long as the blanket repeal is preserved accurately.

laws.title: Public Law 1-4 | Retracting of Resignations Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Section 2(a)-(b) establishes the resignation retraction rule; section 2(c) directs codification under 18 U.S.C. section 918.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; the source text contains a section-reference defect that blocks a fully safe editorial target resolution.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer issue; the obstacle is the internal citation defect in section 2(c).
laws.rationale: The enactment is substantive and permanent, but the operative target cannot be resolved with full certainty because the statute cites a nonexistent section 3(a) while directing codification to section 918.

laws.title: Public Law 2-5 | Judicial Efficiency Act (Keywords: Maximum, Minimum, Sentence, Sentencing)
laws.final_statutory_text: Historical sentence-reform text only; no current operative text remains after repeal.
laws.chronology: Fully repealed by later enactment PL-19-149.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Complete repeal; no surviving transfer or redesignation.
laws.rationale: The later repeal makes note-only treatment sufficient. No operative text should remain.

laws.title: Public Law 2-6 | Shelby-Kabob Act of 2021
laws.final_statutory_text: Historical federal-agent use-of-force text only; no current operative text remains after repeal.
laws.chronology: Fully repealed by later enactment PL-14-89.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Complete repeal; no surviving transfer or redesignation.
laws.rationale: The later repeal removes the need for operative codification. Historical note treatment is sufficient.

laws.title: Public Law 2-7 | Court of Appeals Temporary Suspension Act of 2021
laws.final_statutory_text: Historical temporary-suspension text only; no current operative text remains after repeal.
laws.chronology: Fully repealed by later enactment PL-3-24.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Complete repeal; no surviving transfer or redesignation.
laws.rationale: The law was temporary and has been fully superseded. Note-only treatment is legally sufficient.

laws.title: Public Law 2-8 | National Security Act of 2021
laws.final_statutory_text: National Security Council framework establishing membership, responsibilities, chair powers, and security-threat procedures.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; no later repeal identified in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer; the substantive framework remains live.
laws.rationale: This is an ongoing statutory framework. A note alone does not satisfy the required codification.

laws.title: Public Law 2-10 | Amendment to the District of Columbia City Charter
laws.final_statutory_text: District of Columbia City Charter amendments affecting mayoral and council terms and related charter language.
laws.chronology: Current local-charter amendment; later D.C. enactments affect the same area but do not make this note-only.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Charter amendment requires actual text integration rather than a mere history note.
laws.rationale: The source amends charter text. A statutory note alone is not legally sufficient for the live charter changes.

laws.title: Public Law 2-11 | Criminal Statute of Limitations Act
laws.final_statutory_text: General criminal limitations rule for Title 18 offenses, including longer periods for listed chapters and accrual at offense time.
laws.chronology: Current enactment; no safe exact Code section was resolved in the reviewed packet, and the packet target set was empty.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer issue; the unresolved point is the precise codification anchor.
laws.rationale: The rule is general and permanent, so a note is not enough. The review packet did not resolve a safe exact section target, so the codification anchor remains open.
